plan_metadata.name: Sample Tab And Outlook Addin Remote Debug

steps.description: Type the text 'tab and outlook' into the search input field within the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Create" button next to the "Hello World Teams Tab and Outlook add-in" sample in the "Samples" panel of Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit within Visual Studio Code. And input ${{var:app_name:Contoso}} into the run time variable.
Click the "Deploy" button under the "LIFECYCLE" section in the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT panel within Visual Studio Code to execute the deploy lifecycle stage.
Click the "Deploy" button in the confirmation dialog that asks "Do you want to deploy resources in dev environment?" within Visual Studio Code.
@assertion there's "Ok to proceed? (y)" exist in terminal log.
Click the "View provisioned resources" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit notification panel within Visual Studio Code.
Press Enter key in the VS Code terminal to confirm the prompt "Ok to proceed? (y/n)" during Azure Static Web Apps deployment using the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
Press the Enter key in the VS Code integrated terminal to confirm the "Ok to proceed? (y)" prompt during Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit deployment.
@assertion there's notification: "actions in deploy stage executed successfully." exist
Click the "Build your app locally" link in the main content area of the Microsoft Teams web app under the "tab-with-backenddev" tab.
Scroll to the bottom of the tab content in Microsoft Teams web interface by pressing the End key.
@assertion there's  "Add Single Sign On feature to retrieve user profile" exist.
